rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN B. NELSON VS. BRENDAN M MCGRATH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 23, 2020, line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT JONATHAN NELSON's DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend. No present controversy for which no remedy at law is available has been asserted. The demurer to the causes of action for trespass and encroachment re: standing is overruled. While the moving party cited several cases with favorable language/holdings, none of those cases involved similar facts, i.e. a plaintiff currently out-of-possession seeking damages for trespass/encroachment while in possession. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to join current owners of the property. Any determination in this action regarding the alleged encroachment, which is permanent in nature, implicates the rights of the current owners as is asserted in the Second Amended Cross-Complaint ("Such encroachments substantially diminish the value of the McGrath Property and interferes with the quiet enjoyment by the homeowner and any subsequent owner of the McGrath Property." SAXC, paragraph 45) = (501/REQ)
